steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank must complete "of keen interest to ______ of the University of Toronto." The name "Diane Massam" is a restrictive appositive that uniquely identifies which linguist is meant. A restrictive appositive names one specific person and takes no surrounding punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "linguist: Diane Massam": violates the colon rule — a colon must be preceded by an independent clause, but "of keen interest to linguist" is not a complete sentence. ✗
  • "linguist, Diane Massam,": violates the non-restrictive appositive rule — surrounding the name with commas implies it is optional extra information, but only one specific linguist is meant. ✗
  • "linguist Diane Massam": correct use of a restrictive appositive — no punctuation separates the noun from the name that uniquely identifies it. ✓
  • "linguist, Diane Massam": violates the paired-comma rule — an opening comma without a closing comma creates an asymmetric break that matches no standard appositive pattern. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
